attachment.content:
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll read the relevant files to ground the review in actual code before returning findings.

input.description: List all Go files in handset service

content.content:
1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"os"
6		"time"
7	
8		cli "github.com/jawher/mow.cli"
9		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/protoc-gen-protostrate/protostrate"
10		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/protoc-gen-protostrate/protostrate/interceptor/producer"
11		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/protoc-gen-protostrate/protostrate_tools"
12		protostrate_instrumented "github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-monorepo/pkg/prometheus/protostrate"
13		"golang.org/x/sync/errgroup"
14	
15		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/generated/protos/mobile/v1/events/serviceevents"
16		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tbootstrap"
17		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tlog"
18		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tpubsub"
19		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tstorage/tmongo"
20		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tvars"
21		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/events"
22		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/jobs"
23		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/storage"
24	)
25	
26	func startJobsMode(ctx context.Context, eg *errgroup.Group, app *cli.Cli, cfg *sharedConfig) {
27		kafkaTopic := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
28			Name:   "kafka-topic-out",
29			Desc:   "Kafka topic to publish lifecycle events to",
30			EnvVar: "KAFKA_TOPIC_OUT",
31		})
32	
33		mongoDB, err := tmongo.InitMongoDB(ctx, tmongo.MongoOpts{
34			Url:          *cfg.mongoURL,
35			DatabaseName: *cfg.mongoDBName,
36		})
37		if err != nil {
38			tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "failed to initialise mongodb")
39		}
40	
41		db, err := storage.NewStorage(ctx, *mongoDB, time.Now)
42		if err != nil {
43			tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "failed to create mongo store")
44		}
45	
46		// Kafka producer for lifecycle events
47		kafkaSink := tpubsub.InitAsyncSink(ctx, tpubsub.PubSubSinkOpts{
48			PubSubOpts: tpubsub.PubSubOpts{
49				AppName: appName,
50				Brokers: *cfg.kafkaBrokers,
51				Topic:   *kafkaTopic,
52				Version: *cfg.kafkaVersion,
53			},
54		})
55		defer kafkaSink.Sink.Close()
56	
57		protostrateProd := protostrate.NewProducer(
58			protostrate_tools.NewAsyncSubstrateProducerDestination(kafkaSink.Sink),
59			protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(producer.NewSenderDecoratorInterceptor(appName, tvars.Namespace, "")),
60			protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(protostrate_instrumented.NewProducerMetrics()),
61			protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(producer.NewTokenCorrelationInterceptor()),
62			protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(producer.NewAuditInterceptor()),
63			tpubsub.WithProducerEnvelopeTranslator(),
64		)
65		defer protostrateProd.Close()
66		go func() {
67			if err := protostrateProd.Produce(ctx); err != nil {
68				tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "stopped producing messages")
69			}
70		}()
71	
72		eventProducer := events.NewProducer(serviceevents.NewServiceeventsProducer(protostrateProd))
73	
74		// Setup scheduler for handset updates
75		tlog.Info(ctx, "Setup scheduler for handset updates")
76		jobs := jobs.New(ctx, db, eventProducer)
77		jobs.Start()
78		defer jobs.Stop()
79	
80		opsServer := tbootstrap.InitOpsServer(ctx, tbootstrap.OpsServerOpts{
81			AppName:        appName,
82			AppDescription: appDesc,
83			Revision:       gitHash,
84			Addr:           *cfg.opsAddress,
85		})
86	
87		err = tbootstrap.StartBootstrapGroup(eg,
88			tbootstrap.WithMongoClient(ctx, *mongoDB),
89			tbootstrap.WithProtostrateProducer(ctx, protostrateProd, *kafkaTopic),
90			tbootstrap.WithOpsServer(ctx, opsServer),
91		)
92		if err != nil {
93			tlog.WithError(err).Error(ctx, "error returned from error group")
94			os.Exit(1)
95		}
96	}
97

1	package models
2	
3	import (
4		"time"
5	
6		"github.com/google/uuid"
7	)
8	
9	type ContractStatus string
10	
11	const (
12		ContractStatusPendingEligibility  ContractStatus = "PENDING_ELIGIBILITY"
13		ContractStatusEligibilityApproved ContractStatus = "ELIGIBILITY_APPROVED"
14		ContractStatusApproved            ContractStatus = "APPROVED"
15		ContractStatusDeclined            ContractStatus = "DECLINED"
16		ContractStatusHandsetOrdered      ContractStatus = "HANDSET_ORDERED"
17		ContractStatusExpired             ContractStatus = "EXPIRED"
18	)
19	
20	type Handset struct {
21		ID                uuid.UUID           `json:"id" bson:"id"`
22		BillID            string              `json:"billID" bson:"billID"`
23		Active            bool                `json:"active" bson:"active"`
24		QuantityInStock   int                 `json:"quantityInStock" bson:"quantityInStock"`
25		CurrentProperties HandsetProperties   `json:"currentProperties" bson:"currentProperties"`
26		Versions          []HandsetProperties `json:"versions" bson:"versions"`
27	}
28	
29	type HandsetProperties struct {
30		Version            string    `bson:"version" json:"version"`
31		Name               string    `bson:"name" json:"name"`
32		RentalPricePence   int       `bson:"rentalPricePence" json:"rentalPricePence"`
33		RRPPence           int       `bson:"rrpPence" json:"rrpPence"`
34		ContractTermMonths int       `bson:"contractTermMonths" json:"contractTermMonths"`
35		Manufacturer       string    `bson:"manufacturer" json:"manufacturer"`
36		Model              string    `bson:"model" json:"model"`
37		Colours            string    `bson:"colours" json:"colours"`
38		Capacity           string    `bson:"capacity" json:"capacity"`
39		ScreenSize         string    `bson:"screenSize" json:"screenSize"`
40		Battery            string    `bson:"battery" json:"battery"`
41		SDCapacity         string    `bson:"sdCapacity" json:"sdCapacity"`
42		WiFiStandard       string    `bson:"wiFiStandard" json:"wiFiStandard"`
43		ESim               bool      `bson:"eSim" json:"eSim"`
44		DualSim            bool      `bson:"dualSim" json:"dualSim"`
45		SaleStatus         string    `bson:"saleStatus" json:"saleStatus"`
46		IsNew              bool      `bson:"isNew" json:"isNew"`
47		UWLaunchDate       time.Time `bson:"uwLaunchDate" json:"uwLaunchDate"`
48		PriceChange        bool      `bson:"priceChange" json:"priceChange"`
49		EndOfLife          bool      `bson:"endOfLife" json:"endOfLife"`
50		SpecificationsLink string    `bson:"specificationsLink" json:"specificationsLink"`
51		BillID             string    `bson:"billID" json:"billID"`
52	}
53	
54	type Contract struct {
55		ID                 uuid.UUID  `bson:"id"`
56		ServiceID          string     `bson:"serviceId"`
57		CustomerID         string     `bson:"customerId"`
58		HandsetID          string     `bson:"handsetId"`
59		HandsetName        string     `bson:"handsetName"`
60		HandsetVersion     string     `bson:"handsetVersion"`
61		ContractPrice      string     `bson:"contractPrice"`
62		RetailPrice        string     `bson:"retailPrice"`
63		ContractMonths     string     `bson:"contractMonths"`
64		RequestID          string     `bson:"requestId"`
65		LoanReference      string     `bson:"loanReference,omitempty"`
66		BankAccountNumber  string     `bson:"bankAccountNumber,omitempty"`
67		BankTitle          string     `bson:"bankTitle,omitempty"`
68		Decision           string     `bson:"decision,omitempty"`
69		AgreementID        string     `bson:"agreementId,omitempty"`
70		DocusignEnvelopeID string     `bson:"docusignEnvelopeId,omitempty"`
71		SignedAt           *time.Time `bson:"signedAt,omitempty"`
72		// This field is calculated when handset is ordered
73		ExpiresAt      *time.Time     `bson:"expiresAt,omitempty"`
74		CompletedAt    *time.Time     `bson:"completedAt,omitempty"`
75		FailureReason  string         `bson:"failureReason,omitempty"`
76		FailureStage   string         `bson:"failureStage,omitempty"`
77		FailureMessage string         `bson:"failureMessage,omitempty"`
78		FailedAt       *time.Time     `bson:"failedAt,omitempty"`
79		Status         ContractStatus `bson:"status"`
80		CreatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"createdAt"`
81		UpdatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"updatedAt"`
82	}
83	
84	type EligibilityOutcome struct {
85		Decision           string
86		AgreementID        string
87		DocusignEnvelopeID string
88		SignedAt           *time.Time
89		CompletedAt        *time.Time
90	}
91	
92	type EligibilityFailureOutcome struct {
93		FailureReason  string
94		FailureStage   string
95		FailureMessage string
96		FailedAt       *time.Time
97	}
98

1	package scheduler
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"sync"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tlog"
9	)
10	
11	type JobScheduler interface {
12		Start()
13		Stop()
14	}
15	
16	type Scheduler struct {
17		ticker     *time.Ticker
18		busy       *sync.Mutex
19		completion *sync.WaitGroup
20		function   func(ctx context.Context)
21		done       chan bool
22		ctx        context.Context
23	}
24	
25	func NewScheduler(ctx context.Context, function func(ctx context.Context), interval time.Duration) *Scheduler {
26		ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
27	
28		sched := &Scheduler{
29			ctx:        ctx,
30			ticker:     ticker,
31			busy:       &sync.Mutex{},
32			completion: &sync.WaitGroup{},
33			done:       make(chan bool),
34		}
35	
36		sched.function = function
37	
38		return sched
39	}
40	
41	func (c *Scheduler) Start() {
42		c.completion.Add(1)
43		go func() {
44			defer c.completion.Done()
45			for {
46				select {
47				case <-c.ctx.Done():
48					tlog.Info(c.ctx, "gracefully shutting down scheduler")
49					return
50				case <-c.done:
51					return
52				case <-c.ticker.C:
53					c.completion.Add(1)
54					go c.runTargetFunctionIfNotRunning()
55				}
56			}
57		}()
58	}
59	
60	func (c *Scheduler) Stop() {
61		c.ticker.Stop()
62		close(c.done)
63		c.completion.Wait()
64	}
65	
66	func (c *Scheduler) runTargetFunctionIfNotRunning() {
67		defer c.completion.Done()
68	
69		if !c.busy.TryLock() {
70			return // if the previous processing is still ongoing, we bail out
71		}
72	
73		defer c.busy.Unlock()
74		c.function(c.ctx)
75	}
76

input.description: Check generated pb.go for field naming of MobileHandsetContractExpired

content.text:
Now I have everything I need. Here are the findings:

---

**Bug 1 — `FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts`: double `nowFunc()` call creates a timestamp inconsistency**

File: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go`, lines 400 and 405

The filter is built with one call to `s.nowFunc()` and the `$set` update is built with a second independent call. If `nowFunc` is `time.Now` (as wired in `cmd/handset/jobs.go`), these two calls return slightly different instants. The filter uses T1; `updatedAt` is set to T2 where T2 > T1. This is cosmetically wrong — the stored `updatedAt` is after the cutoff used for matching. Not a correctness bug in isolation, but if anything downstream treats `updatedAt` as "the time we decided it was expired", it will be slightly off. The real fix is to capture `now := s.nowFunc()` once before building both maps.

**Bug 2 — `FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts`: infinite loop if MongoDB keeps returning the same document**

File: `storage.go`, lines 411-421

`FindOneAndUpdate` with `SetReturnDocument(options.After)` returns the document **after** the update — i.e., with `status = EXPIRED`. The filter requires `status = HANDSET_ORDERED`. So once updated, that document no longer matches the filter and won't be returned again. The loop is safe.

This is **not a bug** — the termination guarantee holds.

**Bug 3 — Proto field name mismatch: `expired_at` in proto, `expires_at` in generated code**

File: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/protos/mobile/v1/events/service_events.proto`, line 424; generated pb.go line 3390

The proto source says `expired_at = 5` but the generated `.pb.go` has the field tagged as `name=expires_at` and the struct field is `ExpiresAt`. The serialised wire/JSON name will be `expires_at`, not `expired_at`. Any consumer or schema registry that reads the proto source as the contract and expects `expired_at` will be broken. The generated file and the proto source are out of sync — the proto was likely edited after generation without re-running `protoc`. The Go code in `expired_contracts.go` sets `ExpiresAt` which compiles against the generated file, so the Go side works, but the source of truth (the proto) is wrong and will mislead anyone who reads it.

**Bug 4 — `consumer.go`: `expires_at` permanently lost on partial failure (real bug)**

File: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go`, lines 215-238

Sequence:
1. `UpdateContractStatusByID` succeeds → contract is now `HANDSET_ORDERED`.
2. `UpdateContractExpiresAt` fails (transient DB error) → function returns a non-nil error, message is nacked and redelivered.
3. On retry: `GetContractByID` returns the contract with `status = HANDSET_ORDERED`.
4. The idempotency check at line 205-209 fires and calls `ack()` immediately — skipping the `UpdateContractExpiresAt` call entirely.

Result: `expires_at` is never written. The expiry job's filter `"expiresAt": {"$lte": now}` will never match this contract (null `expiresAt` doesn't satisfy `$lte`), so it silently never expires.

**Bug 5 — `cmd/handset/jobs.go`: `os.Exit(1)` bypasses deferred cleanup**

File: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/cmd/handset/jobs.go`, lines 87-95

`os.Exit(1)` unwinds the OS process without running deferred functions. The deferred calls in scope are:
- `kafkaSink.Sink.Close()` — buffered async Kafka messages are discarded, potentially losing produced events.
- `protostrateProd.Close()` — same concern; the producer's internal goroutine is abandoned.
- `jobs.Stop()` — the scheduler's `Stop()` calls `c.completion.Wait()`, meaning any in-flight expiry run is not given a chance to finish cleanly.

For the Kafka producer this is the most concrete harm: any events that were batched but not yet flushed to the broker are silently dropped.

**Bug 6 — `jobs.New` captures startup context, not runtime context**

File: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/jobs/jobs.go`, line 29; `scheduler.go`, line 29

`jobs.New(ctx, ...)` is called with the function-local `ctx` from `startJobsMode`. That context is passed into `NewScheduler` and stored as `c.ctx` on the `Scheduler`. The scheduler's `Start` goroutine selects on `c.ctx.Done()` for graceful shutdown — so the shutdown signal has to come from that stored context, not from any externally signalled cancellation unless they are the same context. Looking at `cmd/handset/jobs.go`, the `ctx` passed to `startJobsMode` comes from the bootstrap group's context chain. This is likely fine in practice, but the stored context is the *startup* context, meaning if it is cancelled for an unrelated reason early on (e.g. during init), the scheduler silently exits before jobs have run. The safer pattern is to pass a freshly derived context at `Start()` time or use the errgroup context. Low severity, but worth noting.

---

**Summary ranked by severity:**

| # | File | Approx line | Issue |
|---|------|-------------|-------|
| 1 | `consumer.go` | 205-238 | `expires_at` permanently lost if `UpdateContractExpiresAt` fails after status update — real data loss |
| 2 | `cmd/handset/jobs.go` | 93 | `os.Exit(1)` skips deferred Kafka flush and `jobs.Stop()` — in-flight events dropped |
| 3 | `service_events.proto` | 424 | `expired_at` in source vs `expires_at` in generated code — proto source is wrong, will mislead consumers |
| 4 | `storage.go` | 400, 405 | Double `nowFunc()` call makes filter time and `updatedAt` slightly different instants |
| 5 | `jobs.go` | 29 | Scheduler stores startup context; cancellation at wrong time silently stops all jobs |
